content: Daegon: So, pig-faced steel cock, tell me—how's Whiterun these days? Still full of uptight prudes clutching their mead like virgins on their wedding night? Or have things gotten... interesting while I was away? Whiterun Guard: Tryin' to keep the peace with all you murderous fucks running ‘round. Saw a bard get fucked to death by a pack of wolves outside the gate last week. Woulda stepped in, but he was beggin' for more. Skyrim’s gone mad since them dragons started fuckin’ everything that moves. Daegon: Oh, don’t pretend you didn’t enjoy the show. I bet you 'accidentally' dropped your sword and had to watch a while before 'intervening. ' Admit it—nothing gets your fat Nord cock harder than watching nature take its course. Whiterun Guard: Mighta stroked it a bit too if I wasn’t on duty. But that’s nothing compared to what goes down in the Dragonseed brothel. Last time I checked, they had a troll pounding some milk-drinker’s wife while draugr cheered. Whiterun’s keepin’ it classy. Daegon: Ha! And yet I still see you standing here—virgin steel between your legs instead of a warm body. Tell me, tin man... What keeps you from bending some poor farm girl over the stables and reminding her who owns these walls?

Whiterun Guard: Pah! Unlike some of you horny knife-ears, I actually take my oath seriously. The Jarl’s got standards—unlike that drunken shitshow in Windhelm. Daegon: Oh, noble to the end, how . But don’t think I haven’t spotted you eyeing up Irileth’s ass when she barks orders. Everyone cracks eventually, love. Whiterun Guard: Fuck off with your nattering. I’ve got bandit heads to pile up by dawn, and you’ve got whatever plans you filthy witches scheme at this hour. Daegon: Fine, fine. Enjoy your lonely tower full of sad sword-polishing and unspent rage. I’ll be sure to scream loud enough for you when I find a distraction.

(The location is now Dragonsreach.) (Characters nearby (from nearest to furthest): Proventus Avenicci, Lydia, Daegon, Jenassa, Korpse) (MJR: What about my reward?; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: But I haven't forgotten the service you did for me in retrieving the Dragonstone for Farengar. As a token of my esteem, I have instructed Avenicci that you are now permitted to purchase property in the city. And please accept this gift from my personal armory. MJR: ...; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: There's no time to stand on ceremony, my friend. I need your help again. I want you to go with Irileth and help her fight this dragon. You survived Helgen, so you have more experience with dragons than anyone else here. MJR: ...; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: So what happened at the watchtower? Was the dragon there? MJR: Turns out I may be something called "Dragonborn."; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: Dragonborn? What do you know about the Dragonborn? MJR: When the dragon died, I absorbed some kind of power from it.; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: So it's true. The Greybeards really were summoning you. MJR: The Greybeards?; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: Masters of the Way of the Voice. They live in seclusion high on the slopes of the Throat of the World. MJR: What do these Greybeards want with me?; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: The Dragonborn is said to be uniquely gifted in the Voice - the ability to focus your vital essence into a Thu'um, or Shout. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your gift. MJR: ...; Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: You've done a great service for me and my city, Dragonborn.)

Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: But now, with this dragon terrorizing the Western Watchtower, we must act swiftly. The morning sun's barely crested the plains, and already Irileth reports smoke rising from the hills. Whiterun Guard: Aye, Jarl, the air's crisp this early—feels like frost from the tundra still clings to our armor. That beast up there won't wait for us to warm by the braziers. Whiterun Guard 2: I've patrolled these walls since dawn, my lord. The wind's carrying cries from the watchtower; we can't let it reach the city gates. Dragonborn or not, we stand ready. Nelkir: Dragonborn, huh? Father's always going on about ancient prophecies in this stuffy old hall. Bet that whispering door downstairs knows more about it than he lets on—secrets echoing off these stone walls like they do at night.

Jarl Balgruuf the Greater: By Shor, what madness is this? Assassins in my own hall, with the sun barely up and the embers still glowing in the braziers—drag them out and question them before they bleed out on these floors! Whiterun Guard: Jarl, that witch Kaila came at us with poison blades;

(*You are observing the following:* In this dimly lit interior scene, viewed from a low perspective as if standing within the space, the architecture evokes a sturdy, ancient wooden hall. Tall, arched ceilings supported by thick, exposed timber beams rise overhead, their surfaces weathered and darkened with age, interspersed with subtle cross-bracing for structural depth. The walls, constructed from heavy dark wood planks, feature narrow, vertical windows framed by ornate latticework, allowing shafts of soft sunlight to pierce through and cast elongated, patterned shadows across the floor. Prominent pillars, carved with swirling, wave-like motifs resembling Nordic or tribal designs, stand sentinel along the edges of the room, their bases slightly shadowed and textured with grooves. A large, intricate rug dominates the foreground floor, woven in rich blues and golds with geometric borders and central motifs that catch the light, transitioning from the worn wooden planks beneath. The overall atmosphere is one of quiet grandeur, with warm highlights from the incoming light contrasting the pervasive gloom, suggesting a grand hall perhaps used for gatherings or rituals. At the lower edge of the view, a subtle interface overlay appears, featuring a row of small, translucent icons:)
